tasks.title: Worker 'error' event handler only logs to console, leaving the associated task permanently stuck with no user feedback.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: reprocessLive finds worker by _lastTaskId but a worker can hold multiple task IDs, causing incorrect targeting.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: Live lightbox update also performs the RGB-to-RGBA expansion on the main thread inline in the message handler.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: RGB-to-RGBA conversion (3-channel to 4-channel expansion) runs on the main thread inside drawCanvas, blocking the UI for large images.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: contrast-boost adds 0.15 to the normalized contrast value, potentially exceeding the documented [-1, +1] range.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: Pool _onMessage does not handle 'error_live' type for the lightbox â€” it invokes _liveHandler but the handler at line 402 only checks for 'lightbox_live'.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: tasks.delete(id) on 'done' before the worker is definitively freed can leave orphan tasks if lightbox never arrived.
tasks.defer_reason: Verified at main.js lines 322-324: the comment 'Worker already freed on lightbox' documents the design assumption. The worker protocol in worker.js always emits `lightbox` before `done` (lines 204-226 show `lightbox` is posted synchronously before the `await encode_jxl` that leads to `done`). The scenario requires worker.js to be modified to skip `lightbox`, which doesn't exist today. A defensive check (`if (!t.released) this._releaseWorker(worker, id)`) would be good practice but addresses only a hypothetical future change.

tasks.title: _releaseWorker unconditionally pushes the worker to free[] even if it is already there, risking double-dispatch.
tasks.defer_reason: Verified: `_release` is defined at lines 347-350 but has no call sites in the current main.js â€” it is an unused method. The double-push risk exists only if `_release` is called on an already-released task, which cannot happen with the current code. The inconsistency between `_release` (no `t.released` guard) and the error handler (has guard) is a maintenance hazard worth noting but is not an active bug.

tasks.title: liveInFlight flag and livePendingLook state are not coordinated with lightbox navigation, causing stale re-renders.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: canvas.width and canvas.height are reassigned on every drawCanvas call, clearing the canvas and forcing a layout recalculation even when dimensions have not changed.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: Object URL created for the JXL download link is never revoked when the card is re-processed, causing a memory leak.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: applyLookBtn selects all cards then calls reprocessSelected, but selection state is not restored afterward.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: triggerGalleryLiveUpdate calls cards.filter() and c._taskId lookups on every debounced slider event.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: card.querySelector() is called multiple times in the onThumb/onDone callbacks for each file, inside async closures.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: JXL blob URL used for the inline download link has no revocation on lightbox close or page unload.
tasks.defer_reason: Verified at main.js lines 1133-1143: the 30-second `setTimeout(revokeObjectURL)` is present and will eventually clean up each URL. The concern (multiple rapid clicks accumulating blobs) is real but requires deliberate abuse; in normal use, one click triggers one download and the URL is revoked 30 seconds later. The fix (revoke immediately after `.click()`) would be an improvement but no significant leak exists under normal usage.

tasks.title: handleFileList is declared async but is called without await, discarding its Promise and any thrown errors.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: handleFileList has no deduplication â€” the same ORF file dropped twice creates two separate cards.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: File type filtering uses only a filename extension check, not MIME type or magic-byte validation.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: liveState always points to the most-recently processed file, making reprocess_live silently operate on the wrong image when a worker handles multiple sequential files.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: WASM init failure surfaces as a caught error in the pipeline try/catch, but the wasmReady promise is cached in a rejected state and all subsequent calls will also fail.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: Worker message handler falls through to the full ORF pipeline branch for any unknown message type, including future protocol extensions.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: reprocess_thumb_live silently swallows errors per taskId with an empty catch, providing no signal for systematic failures.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: take_rgb() is called once on result, but fullRgb is then passed to both downscale_rgb AND rgb_to_rgba â€” the buffer may be invalidated after the first WASM call.
tasks.defer_reason: The current code is safe because the generated WASM binding's `take_rgb()` returns a `.slice()` (verified by the finding's reference to line 178 of the generated JS). The multiple-use pattern is valid today. The risk is entirely contingent on wasm-bindgen changing `.slice()` to a view in a future version â€” a reasonable maintenance concern but not a current bug.

tasks.title: thumbRgb and bigRgb are sent as transferable buffers, but fullRgb is retained in the worker for rgb_to_rgba â€” causing an extra full-res copy.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: rgb_to_rgba result is wrapped in Uint8ClampedArray by copy â€” a zero-copy path using the buffer directly would save an allocation.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet
